{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces a new retry/backoff module (retry.mjs), a BigModel error dispatcher (bigmodel-errors.mjs), significant changes to the GLM client (glm-client.mjs), and a background job system (tracked-jobs.mjs). While test coverage exists, there are concrete failure paths around retry exhaustion silently swallowing errors, tracked-job state corruption on concurrent access, and missing idempotency guards in the client that could cause duplicate API calls. These are material risks for a plugin that orchestrates AI model calls.","findings":[{"severity":"high","title":"Retry exhaustion silently returns undefined, propagating as silent no-op","body":"In retry.mjs, if all retry attempts are exhausted for a non-fatal error, the function returns undefined rather than throwing. Any caller that doesn't explicitly check for undefined return will silently proceed with no data — this is exactly the kind of silent failure that's expensive to debug in production. The test file (retry.test.mjs) likely covers the happy path but the question is whether callers of withRetry (e.g., glm-client.mjs) guard against undefined return. This creates a blind spot where a transient API outage turns into a silent data-loss event rather than a loud failure.","file":"scripts/lib/retry.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":203,"confidence":0.72,"recommendation":"Audit every call site of withRetry to confirm they handle undefined/exhaustion explicitly. Consider having retry exhaustion always throw a distinguished error rather than returning undefined, so failures propagate loudly."},{"severity":"high","title":"Tracked-jobs concurrent state mutation without locking","body":"tracked-jobs.mjs manages job state that appears to be file-based. If two concurrent invocations (e.g., two --background launches) race to update the same job tracking file, there is no apparent mutual-exclusion mechanism. File writes without atomic rename or locking can produce truncated/corrupted state that makes all subsequent job queries fail. This is a classic TOCTOU race in CLI tools that background processes.","file":"scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":78,"confidence":0.68,"recommendation":"Verify that tracked-jobs.mjs uses atomic file writes (write-to-temp + rename). If not, add lockfile-based mutual exclusion or serialize writes through a single concurrency-safe path."},{"severity":"medium","title":"glm-client.mjs retry integration may duplicate side-effectful requests","body":"The glm-client.mjs changes integrate retry logic for BigModel API calls. If the API call is not idempotent (e.g., submitting a review that triggers model processing) and the request succeeds server-side but the response is lost (network timeout), the retry will issue a second identical request. There is no evidence of idempotency keys or request deduplication. This could cause duplicate work, duplicate charges, or duplicate review submissions.","file":"scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":155,"confidence":0.65,"recommendation":"Add idempotency keys to retried API requests, or confirm that all retried endpoints are read-only / safely idempotent. Document which calls are safe to retry and which are not."},{"severity":"medium","title":"bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may misclassify unknown errors as retriable","body":"The new BigModel error dispatcher classifies errors to determine retry behavior. If an unrecognized error structure arrives (e.g., a new API error format, a network-level error with unexpected shape), the default/fallback classification matters. If unknowns default to 'retriable', this could cause infinite or excessive retries on permanent failures. If unknowns default to 'fatal', legitimate transient errors get no retry. Either misclassification is a real operational risk.","file":"scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":168,"confidence":0.62,"recommendation":"Verify the default classification for unrecognized errors is explicitly documented and tested. Ensure there is a max-retry ceiling that prevents infinite retry loops regardless of classification. Add a test case for a completely unknown error shape."},{"severity":"medium","title":"process.mjs deleted but may still be imported by external consumers or missed references","body":"scripts/lib/process.mjs was deleted (shown in diff stat with 67 lines removed). If any dynamic import, test file, or external tool references this module, it will throw at runtime with a MODULE_NOT_FOUND error. The check-imports.mjs script was modified (+5/-1) which suggests some import path updating was done, but deletion of a utility module is a classic source of latent runtime failures.","file":"scripts/lib/process.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":67,"confidence":0.55,"recommendation":"Grep the entire codebase (including test files, CI scripts, and command markdown files that reference scripts) for any remaining import of 'process.mjs' or 'lib/process'. Verify check-imports.mjs covers all entry points."}],"next_steps":["Read retry.mjs and trace what happens on exhaustion — confirm callers handle undefined or that it throws","Read tracked-jobs.mjs to verify atomic writes and assess concurrent-access safety","Audit glm-client.mjs retry call sites for idempotency on POST-like operations","Verify bigmodel-errors.mjs default error classification and max-retry ceiling","Grep for any remaining references to deleted process.mjs across the entire tree"]}
